examples.title: Waste Segregation Algorithm
examples.description: Procedure for proper segregation of biomedical waste at point of generation
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SegregateWaste(item)
BEGIN
 IF item contains blood/body fluids OR is culture/media THEN
 PLACE in Yellow Bin
 ELSE IF item is needle/syringe/blade/sharp glass THEN
 PLACE in Blue/White Puncture-proof Container
 ELSE IF item is plastic/glass recyclable (gloves, mask, tube) THEN
 PLACE in Red Bin
 ELSE
 PLACE in Black Bin (General Waste)
 ENDIF
 RECORD waste type and bin color
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm guides healthcare workers to correctly segregate waste at source based on the BMW Rules 2016 color-coding system.

examples.title: Autoclave Cycle for Infectious Waste
examples.description: Steam sterilization process for treating infectious biomedical waste
examples.code:
PROCEDURE AutoclaveTreatment(wasteContainer)
BEGIN
 LOAD waste into autoclave chamber
 SET temperature to 121°C
 SET pressure to 15 psi (103 kPa)
 START cycle timer for 30 minutes
 
 WHILE cycle running DO
 MONITOR temperature and pressure
 IF temperature < 121°C OR pressure < 15 psi THEN
 ADJUST steam supply
 ENDIF
 ENDWHILE
 
 COMPLETE cycle when timer reaches 30 minutes
 ALLOW chamber to cool to 80°C
 REMOVE treated waste
 MARK container as "TREATED"
 STORE in designated area for disposal
END
examples.explanation: Autoclaving is the gold standard for treating infectious waste. The process uses saturated steam at 121°C and 15 psi for 30 minutes to achieve sterilization.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is cycle time
